page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The processor includes 33 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512, AES cryptography, and BMI2. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the average tier for most general compute and memory operations. It achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 15685.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 44587.75. Notably, it demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads, scoring 40780.0 in software compilation with Clang and 14883.0 in image processing, both placing in the top 25% of benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is measured at 3253.46 GB/sec for cached small blocks, and 162.21 GB/sec for uncached large block reads.

For specialized tasks, the server processes Redis SET operations at 53.97 million operations per second and handles static web serving throughput at 36.1 GB/sec. Large-scale LLM inference reveals architectural limitations, with text generation for a 70B model dropping to a weak rating of 2.56 tokens per second, though smaller 135M models run at 193.61 tokens per second. This profile makes the n2-highmem-64 highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads like database operations, software compilation, and image processing, while being less optimal for large-scale LLM text generation without GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-64 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 32 physical cores. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Benchmark results indicate solid mid-range performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 44587.75 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15685.0. It excels in software compilation and image processing, ranking in the top 25% for these workloads. However, it exhibits weaker performance in large-scale LLM text generation, achieving only 2.56 tokens per second on a 70B model. With a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, this instance is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and parallel build systems.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It delivers strong performance in software compilation and database operations.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, ideal for databases and software compilation.
tagline: A high-memory x86_64 server featuring 512 GB RAM and 64 vCPUs, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM providing a 1:8 core-to-memory ratio
Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz with 32 physical cores
Strong performance in software compilation and image processing workloads
Average performance across standard CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, build systems, and web serving
